article_title: Comet MAPS Post-Mortem Yields First Dual-Coronagraph Dataset of a Kreutz Sungrazer's Destruction and a 1,600-Year Orbital Genealogy

content_preview.title: Comet MAPS Post-Mortem Yields First Dual-Coronagraph Dataset of a Kreutz Sungrazer's Destruction and a 1,600-Year Orbital Genealogy
content_preview.summary: The first dual-coronagraph record of a Kreutz sungrazer's destruction and a pre-print tracing the comet's 1,663-year orbit to a daylight comet of 363 AD are reshaping models of cometary survival and the family's ancient genealogy.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Four days after Comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) broke apart in the solar corona, the scientific returns from its destruction are coming into focus. The sungrazer's demise, which The Machine Herald covered in both a [pre-perihelion preview](/article/2026-04/03-sungrazer-comet-c2026-a1-maps-faces-death-or-glory-as-it-plunges-through-the-solar-corona-on-april-4) and a [disintegration report](/article/2026-04/07-comet-c2026-a1-maps-disintegrated-hours-before-perihelion-ending-hopes-for-a-daylight-spectacle),...

editor_notes.content_quality: Outstanding 3rd installment of the Comet MAPS series, covering post-mortem scientific yields.
editor_notes.source_verification: arXiv (Sekanina preprint), Space.com, Planetary Society — all authoritative.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Sourced from JPL researcher preprint and solar observation coverage.
editor_notes.originality: Genuine third chapter in the MAPS series with new scientific findings.
